```{r subject-level-plots, fig.height=10, fig.width=12, fig.keep='all'}
      ggplot(aes(x = observed, y = predicted)) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
      geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "blue") +
      geom_text(
        data = model_stats %>% 
              mutate(model_display = factor(model_display, levels = model_display_order)),
        aes(
          # Position at top-left of each panel
          x = min(all_results$observed, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.05,
          y = max(all_results$predicted, na.rm = TRUE) - 0.05,
          label = sprintf("r = %.3f\nRMSE = %.3f", correlation, rmse)
        ),
        hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = 3
      ) +
      facet_wrap(~ model_display) +
      labs(
        title = paste("Subject-Level Predictions for", metric_name, "Across Models"),
        subtitle = "Models ordered by correlation (higher is better)",
        x = "Observed Value",
        y = "Predicted Value"
      ) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11)
      )
    
    return(result_plot)
  } else {
    return(NULL)
  }
}

# Create comparisons for key metrics across models
# RL Metrics
rl_metrics_to_plot <- c("good_deck_ratio", "new_good_deck_ratio", "total_money")

# Select a subset of models for better readability
selected_models <- c(
  # Include a mix of RL model types
  "pvldelta", "pvldelta_ddm", 
  "pvldecay", "pvldecay_ddm",
  "ev", "ev_ddm",
  "vppdeltag", "vppdeltag_ddm"
)

# Create comparison plots for RL metrics
for (metric in rl_metrics_to_plot) {
  cat(paste("\n### Cross-Model Comparison for", metric, "\n"))
  comparison_plot <- create_metric_comparison(metric, selected_models, "rl")
  if (!is.null(comparison_plot)) {
    print(comparison_plot)
    # Clean up
    rm(comparison_plot)
    gc()
  } else {
    cat("No data available for multi-model comparison\n")
  }
}

# RT Metrics
rt_metrics_to_plot <- c("rt_play_mean", "rt_skip_mean", "choice_prob")
rt_models_to_compare <- intersect(available_hybrid_models, selected_models)

# Create comparison plots for RT metrics
for (metric in rt_metrics_to_plot) {
  cat(paste("\n### Cross-Model Comparison for", metric, "\n"))
  comparison_plot <- create_metric_comparison(metric, rt_models_to_compare, "ssm")
  if (!is.null(comparison_plot)) {
    print(comparison_plot)
    # Clean up
    rm(comparison_plot)
    gc()
  } else {
    cat("No data available for multi-model comparison\n")
  }
}
```

## Individual Differences Capture

```{r individual-diff-capture, fig.height=10, fig.width=14, fig.keep='all'}
# Function to calculate individual difference correlations
calculate_individual_correlations <- function(model_name, metrics, component = "rl") {
  if (!model_name %in% names(ppc_data)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Initialize results dataframe
  results <- data.frame()
  
  # Process each metric
  for (metric_name in metrics) {
    # Get subject-level data
    scatter_data <- data.frame()
    
    # Get all subjects
    subject_ids <- names(ppc_data[[model_name]]$subjects)
    
    # Iterate through subjects
    for (subject_id in subject_ids) {
      subject_data <- ppc_data[[model_name]]$subjects[[subject_id]]
      
      # Check access path based on component
      if (component == "rl") {
        # For RL-only models or RL component of hybrid models
        if ("summary" %in% names(subject_data) && 
            (is.data.frame(subject_data$summary) || 
             (is.list(subject_data$summary) && "rl" %in% names(subject_data$summary)))) {
          
          # Get the summary data
          if (is.data.frame(subject_data$summary)) {
            summary_df <- subject_data$summary
          } else {
            summary_df <- subject_data$summary$rl
          }
          
          # Find the row for this metric
          metric_row <- summary_df[summary_df$statistic == metric_name, ]
          
          if (nrow(metric_row) > 0) {
            # Extract observed and predicted values
            observed <- metric_row$observed
            predicted <- metric_row$predicted_mean
            
            # Add to dataframe
            scatter_data <- bind_rows(scatter_data, 
                                     data.frame(
                                       subject_id = subject_id,
                                       observed = observed,
                                       predicted = predicted
                                     ))
          }
        }
      } else if (component == "ssm") {
        # For DDM-only models or SSM component of hybrid models
        if ("summary" %in% names(subject_data) && 
            (is.data.frame(subject_data$summary) || 
             (is.list(subject_data$summary) && "ssm" %in% names(subject_data$summary)))) {
          
          # Get the summary data
          if (is.data.frame(subject_data$summary)) {
            summary_df <- subject_data$summary
          } else {
            summary_df <- subject_data$summary$ssm
          }
          
          # Find the row for this metric
          metric_row <- summary_df[summary_df$statistic == metric_name, ]
          
          if (nrow(metric_row) > 0) {
            # Extract observed and predicted values
            observed <- metric_row$observed
            predicted <- metric_row$predicted_mean
            
            # Add to dataframe
            scatter_data <- bind_rows(scatter_data, 
                                     data.frame(
                                       subject_id = subject_id,
                                       observed = observed,
                                       predicted = predicted
                                     ))
          }
        }
      }
    }
    
    # Calculate correlation and RMSE if we have data
    if (nrow(scatter_data) > 0) {
      correlation <- cor(scatter_data$observed, scatter_data$predicted, 
                        use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
      rmse <- sqrt(mean((scatter_data$observed - scatter_data$predicted)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
      
      # Calculate bootstrap CI for correlation if sufficient data
      if (nrow(scatter_data) >= 10) {
        boot_correlation <- function(data, indices) {
          cor(data$observed[indices], data$predicted[indices], use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
        }
        
        boot_result <- boot(scatter_data, boot_correlation, R = 1000)
        ci <- boot.ci(boot_result, type = "perc")
        ci_lower <- ci$percent[4]
        ci_upper <- ci$percent[5]
      } else {
        ci_lower <- NA
        ci_upper <- NA
      }
      
      # Add to results
      results <- bind_rows(results, 
                          data.frame(
                            model = model_name,
                            metric = metric_name,
                            correlation = correlation,
                            ci_lower = ci_lower,
                            ci_upper = ci_upper,
                            rmse = rmse,
                            n_subjects = nrow(scatter_data)
                          ))
    }
  }
  
  # Add model display name
  if (nrow(results) > 0) {
    results$model_display <- as.character(unlist(model_display_names[model_name]))
    results$component <- component
  }
  
  return(results)
}

# Calculate individual difference correlations for RL metrics
rl_metrics <- c("good_deck_ratio", "new_good_deck_ratio", "total_money", "avg_outcome", "skip_ratio")
rl_models <- available_all_models  # Use all available models

rl_indiv_correlations <- bind_rows(
  lapply(rl_models, function(model) {
    calculate_individual_correlations(model, rl_metrics, "rl")
  })
)

# Calculate individual difference correlations for RT metrics
rt_metrics <- c("rt_play_mean", "rt_skip_mean", "choice_prob")
rt_models <- c(available_hybrid_models, available_ddm_models)  # Use all hybrid and DDM models

rt_indiv_correlations <- bind_rows(
  lapply(rt_models, function(model) {
    calculate_individual_correlations(model, rt_metrics, "ssm")
  })
)

# Combine all individual difference correlations
all_indiv_correlations <- bind_rows(rl_indiv_correlations, rt_indiv_correlations)

# Create a nicely formatted metrics column
all_indiv_correlations <- all_indiv_correlations %>%
  mutate(
    metric_display = case_when(
      metric == "good_deck_ratio" ~ "Good Deck Ratio",
      metric == "new_good_deck_ratio" ~ "New Good Deck Ratio",
      metric == "new_bad_deck_ratio" ~ "New Bad Deck Ratio",
      metric == "total_money" ~ "Total Money",
      metric == "avg_outcome" ~ "Average Outcome",
      metric == "skip_ratio" ~ "Skip Ratio",
      metric == "rt_play_mean" ~ "Play RT (Mean)",
      metric == "rt_skip_mean" ~ "Skip RT (Mean)",
      metric == "choice_prob" ~ "Choice Probability",
      TRUE ~ metric
    )
  )

# Display summary table
if (nrow(all_indiv_correlations) > 0) {
  kable(all_indiv_correlations %>% 
          select(model_display, component, metric_display, correlation, ci_lower, ci_upper, rmse, n_subjects), 
        digits = 3,
        col.names = c("Model", "Component", "Metric", "Correlation", "95% CI Lower", "95% CI Upper", "RMSE", "# Subjects"),
        caption = "Individual Difference Correlations") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), 
                  full_width = FALSE)
  
  # Create visualization for RL metrics
  rl_indiv_plot <- ggplot(all_indiv_correlations %>% filter(component == "RL"), 
       aes(x = metric_display, y = correlation, fill = model_display)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
                width = 0.25) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(name = "Model") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Individual Difference Prediction: RL Metrics",
       subtitle = "Correlation between observed and predicted values (with 95% confidence intervals)",
       x = "Metric",
       y = "Correlation") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10)
  )
  print(rl_indiv_plot)
  
  # Create visualization for RT metrics
  ssm_indiv_plot <- ggplot(all_indiv_correlations %>% filter(component == "SSM"), 
       aes(x = metric_display, y = correlation, fill = model_display)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
                width = 0.25) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(name = "Model") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Individual Difference Prediction: RT Metrics",
       subtitle = "Correlation between observed and predicted values (with 95% confidence intervals)",
       x = "Metric",
       y = "Correlation") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10)
  )
  print(ssm_indiv_plot)
  
  # Find top-performing models for each metric
  top_models <- all_indiv_correlations %>%
    group_by(metric, metric_display, component) %>%
    slice_max(order_by = correlation, n = 1) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    select(metric, metric_display, component, model_display, correlation, rmse)
  
  # Display top models by metric
  kable(top_models, digits = 3,
        col.names = c("Metric ID", "Metric", "Component", "Best Model", "Correlation", "RMSE"),
        caption = "Top-Performing Models by Metric for Individual Difference Prediction") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), 
                  full_width = FALSE)
  
  # Create a heatmap of individual difference correlations
  indiv_corr_heatmap <- all_indiv_correlations %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = metric_display, y = model_display, fill = correlation)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white") +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", correlation)), color = "black", size = 2.5) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "red", mid = "yellow", high = "green", 
                       midpoint = 0.5, limits = c(0, 1)) +
    facet_wrap(~ component, scales = "free", ncol = 1) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
    labs(title = "Individual Difference Correlations Heatmap",
         subtitle = "By model and metric (higher values indicate better prediction of individual differences)",
         x = "Metric",
         y = "Model",
         fill = "Correlation") +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
      strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 10)
    )
  print(indiv_corr_heatmap)
}
```

# Integrated Model Assessment

## Combined Recovery and PPC Performance

```{r combined-assessment, fig.height=12, fig.width=14, fig.keep='all'}
# Create dataframes for component-specific model performance
create_component_performance <- function(data, component_type) {
  data %>%
    filter(component == component_type) %>%
    group_by(model, model_display) %>%
    summarize(
      mean_ppp = mean(mean_ppp, na.rm = TRUE),
      extreme_ratio = mean(extreme_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
      problematic_stats = sum(extreme_ratio > 0.3, na.rm = TRUE),
      total_stats = n(),
      pct_problematic = problematic_stats / total_stats * 100,
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    rename_with(~ paste0(component_type, "_", .), .cols = c(mean_ppp, extreme_ratio, problematic_stats, total_stats, pct_problematic))
}

# Create data frames for component-specific model performance
rl_component_performance <- create_component_performance(all_summaries, "RL")
ssm_component_performance <- create_component_performance(all_summaries, "SSM")

# Create a base dataframe with all model information
all_models_df <- tibble(
  model = available_all_models,
  model_display = unlist(model_display_names[available_all_models])
)

# Ensure consistent data types for joining
rl_component_performance <- rl_component_performance %>%
  mutate(model_display = as.character(model_display))

ssm_component_performance <- ssm_component_performance %>%
  mutate(model_display = as.character(model_display))

# Join the component performances
model_performance <- all_models_df %>%
  left_join(rl_component_performance, by = c("model", "model_display")) %>%
  left_join(ssm_component_performance, by = c("model", "model_display")) %>%
  mutate(
    # Add model type
    model_type = case_when(
      model %in% rl_only_models ~ "RL Only",
      model %in% ddm_only_models ~ "DDM Only",
      model %in% hybrid_models ~ "Hybrid (RL+DDM)",
      TRUE ~ "Unknown"
    ),
    
    # Calculate overall metrics for all models
    overall_mean_ppp = case_when(
      model %in% rl_only_models ~ RL_mean_ppp,
      model %in% ddm_only_models ~ SSM_mean_ppp,
      model %in% hybrid_models ~ (RL_mean_ppp + SSM_mean_ppp) / 2,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    overall_extreme_ratio = case_when(
      model %in% rl_only_models ~ RL_extreme_ratio,
      model %in% ddm_only_models ~ SSM_extreme_ratio,
      model %in% hybrid_models ~ (RL_extreme_ratio + SSM_extreme_ratio) / 2,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(model_type, overall_extreme_ratio)

# Get parameter recovery performance
param_recovery <- overall_wide %>%
  select(model, correlation, rmse, bias, relative_bias)

# Combine with PPC performance
combined_performance <- model_performance %>%
  left_join(param_recovery, by = "model") %>%
  select(model, model_display, model_type, 
         correlation, rmse, 
         overall_mean_ppp, overall_extreme_ratio,
         RL_mean_ppp, RL_extreme_ratio,
         SSM_mean_ppp, SSM_extreme_ratio)

# Display the combined table
kable(combined_performance, digits = 3,
      col.names = c("Model", "Display Name", "Type", 
                    "Param Correlation", "Param RMSE", 
                    "Overall PPP", "Overall Extreme Ratio",
                    "RL Mean PPP", "RL Extreme Ratio",
                    "SSM Mean PPP", "SSM Extreme Ratio"),
      caption = "Combined Parameter Recovery and PPC Performance") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), 
                full_width = FALSE)

# Create scatter plot of parameter recovery vs. PPC performance
recovery_ppc_scatter <- combined_performance %>%
  mutate(
    ppp_quality = abs(overall_mean_ppp - 0.5),  # Lower is better
    ppp_score = 1 - ppp_quality * 2  # Scale to [0,1], higher is better
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = correlation, y = ppp_score, 
             color = model_type, shape = model_type, 
             label = model_display)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3, box.padding = 0.5, max.overlaps = 30) +
  scale_color_manual(values = model_type_colors) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(16, 17, 15)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Model Performance: Parameter Recovery vs. PPC",
       subtitle = "Better models appear in the upper-right corner",
       x = "Parameter Recovery Correlation (higher is better)", 
       y = "PPC Score (higher is better)",
       color = "Model Type",
       shape = "Model Type") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10)
  )
print(recovery_ppc_scatter)

# Create a similar plot using extreme ratio
extreme_ratio_scatter <- combined_performance %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = correlation, y = 1 - overall_extreme_ratio, 
             color = model_type, shape = model_type, 
             label = model_display)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3, box.padding = 0.5, max.overlaps = 30) +
  scale_color_manual(values = model_type_colors) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(16, 17, 15)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Model Performance: Parameter Recovery vs. PPC",
       subtitle = "Better models appear in the upper-right corner",
       x = "Parameter Recovery Correlation (higher is better)", 
       y = "1 - Extreme Ratio (higher is better)",
       color = "Model Type",
       shape = "Model Type") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10)
  )
print(extreme_ratio_scatter)

# Create a faceted version by model type
faceted_model_scatter <- combined_performance %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = correlation, y = 1 - overall_extreme_ratio, 
             color = model_display, 
             label = model_display)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3, box.padding = 0.5, max.overlaps = 10) +
  facet_wrap(~ model_type) +
  scale_color_viridis_d() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Model Performance by Type: Parameter Recovery vs. PPC",
       subtitle = "Better models appear in the upper-right corner",
       x = "Parameter Recovery Correlation (higher is better)", 
       y = "1 - Extreme Ratio (higher is better)",
       color = "Model") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.position = "none"
  )
print(faceted_model_scatter)

# Create a comprehensive ranking of models
model_ranking <- combined_performance %>%
  # Create standardized scores (z-scores)
  mutate(
    # For parameter recovery, higher correlation is better, lower RMSE is better
    z_param_corr = scale(correlation)[,1],
    z_param_rmse = -scale(rmse)[,1],  # Negative so higher is better
    
    # For PPC, closer to 0.5 is better, so use negative absolute deviation
    z_ppp_quality = -scale(abs(overall_mean_ppp - 0.5))[,1],
    z_extreme_ratio = -scale(overall_extreme_ratio)[,1],  # Lower extreme ratio is better
    
    # Overall parameter recovery score
    param_recovery_score = (z_param_corr + z_param_rmse) / 2,
    
    # Overall PPC score
    ppc_score = (z_ppp_quality + z_extreme_ratio) / 2,
    
    # Total score
    total_score = (param_recovery_score + ppc_score) / 2
  ) %>%
  # Add rank variables
  arrange(desc(total_score)) %>%
  mutate(
    overall_rank = row_number(),
    param_rank = rank(-param_recovery_score),
    ppc_rank = rank(-ppc_score)
  ) %>%
  # Sort by overall rank
  arrange(overall_rank)

# Display the rankings
kable(model_ranking %>% 
        select(model_display, model_type, overall_rank, param_rank, ppc_rank, 
               total_score, param_recovery_score, ppc_score), 
      digits = 2,
      col.names = c("Model", "Type", "Overall Rank", "Param Rank", "PPC Rank", 
                   "Total Score", "Param Score", "PPC Score"),
      caption = "Model Ranking Based on Standardized Performance Scores") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), 
                full_width = FALSE)

# Visualize the rankings
model_ranking_plot <- ggplot(model_ranking, 
       aes(x = param_recovery_score, y = ppc_score, 
           color = model_type, shape = model_type, 
           label = model_display)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3, box.padding = 0.5, max.overlaps = 30) +
  # Add diagonal line for balanced models
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray") +
  scale_color_manual(values = model_type_colors) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(16, 17, 15)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Model Performance: Parameter Recovery vs. PPC Scores",
       subtitle = "Better models appear in the upper-right corner",
       x = "Parameter Recovery Score (standardized)", 
       y = "PPC Score (standardized)",
       color = "Model Type",
       shape = "Model Type") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10)
  )
print(model_ranking_plot)

# Create a comprehensive heatmap of all performance metrics
performance_heatmap_data <- combined_performance %>%
  select(model_display, model_type, correlation, rmse, 
         RL_mean_ppp, RL_extreme_ratio,
         SSM_mean_ppp, SSM_extreme_ratio) %>%
  mutate(
    # Transform metrics so higher is always better
    correlation = correlation,  # already higher is better
    rmse_inv = 1 / rmse,  # invert RMSE so higher is better
    RL_ppp_quality = 1 - 2 * abs(RL_mean_ppp - 0.5),  # transform to 0-1, higher is better
    RL_fit_quality = 1 - RL_extreme_ratio,  # invert extreme ratio
    SSM_ppp_quality = if_else(is.na(SSM_mean_ppp), NA_real_, 1 - 2 * abs(SSM_mean_ppp - 0.5)),
    SSM_fit_quality = if_else(is.na(SSM_extreme_ratio), NA_real_, 1 - SSM_extreme_ratio)
  ) %>%
  # Gather to long format for heatmap
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(correlation, rmse_inv, 
             RL_ppp_quality, RL_fit_quality,
             SSM_ppp_quality, SSM_fit_quality),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "score"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Create readable metric labels
    metric_label = case_when(
      metric == "correlation" ~ "Parameter Recovery\nCorrelation",
      metric == "rmse_inv" ~ "Parameter Recovery\n1/RMSE",
      metric == "RL_ppp_quality" ~ "RL PPP Quality",
      metric == "RL_fit_quality" ~ "RL % Good Fit",
      metric == "SSM_ppp_quality" ~ "RT PPP Quality",
      metric == "SSM_fit_quality" ~ "RT % Good Fit",
      TRUE ~ metric
    ),
    # Order factors
    metric_label = factor(metric_label, levels = c(
      "Parameter Recovery\nCorrelation", "Parameter Recovery\n1/RMSE",
      "RL PPP Quality", "RL % Good Fit",
      "RT PPP Quality", "RT % Good Fit"
    ))
  )

# Create heatmap of all performance metrics
performance_heatmap <- ggplot(performance_heatmap_data, 
       aes(x = metric_label, y = model_display, fill = score)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", score)), 
            color = "black", size = 2.5) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "red", mid = "yellow", high = "green", 
                     midpoint = 0.5, na.value = "gray90") +
  facet_wrap(~ model_type, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Comprehensive Model Performance Heatmap",
       subtitle = "All metrics transformed so higher values (green) are better",
       x = "Performance Metric",
       y = "Model",
       fill = "Score\n(higher is better)") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0)
  )
print(performance_heatmap)
```

## Model-Specific Analysis

```{r model-specific, fig.height=10, fig.width=14, fig.keep='all'}
# Get the top-performing models from each category
top_models <- model_ranking %>%
  group_by(model_type) %>%
  slice_min(order_by = overall_rank, n = 2) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  pull(model)

# Get parameter recovery details for top models
top_model_params <- params_df %>%
  filter(model %in% top_models) %>%
  select(model, model_display, parameter, param_type, correlation, rmse) %>%
  arrange(model, param_type, parameter)

# Display parameter recovery for top models
kable(top_model_params, digits = 3,
      caption = "Parameter Recovery for Top-Performing Models") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), 
                full_width = FALSE)

# Create a visualization of parameter recovery for top models
top_model_param_plot <- ggplot(top_model_params, 
       aes(x = parameter, y = correlation, fill = model_display)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~ param_type, scales = "free_x") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Parameter Recovery for Top-Performing Models",
       subtitle = "Grouped by parameter type",
       x = "Parameter",
       y = "Correlation",
       fill = "Model") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10)
  )
print(top_model_param_plot)

# Get PPC performance for top models
top_model_ppc <- all_summaries %>%
  filter(model %in% top_models) %>%
  group_by(model, model_display, component) %>%
  summarize(
    mean_ppp = mean(mean_ppp, na.rm = TRUE),
    abs_dev = mean(abs(mean_ppp - 0.5), na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_extreme_ratio = mean(extreme_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Display PPC performance for top models
kable(top_model_ppc, digits = 3,
      caption = "PPC Performance for Top-Performing Models") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), 
                full_width = FALSE)

# Create a visualization of PPC performance for top models
top_model_ppc_plot <- ggplot(top_model_ppc, 
       aes(x = abs_dev, y = 1 - mean_extreme_ratio, color = model_display)) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = model_display), box.padding = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ component, scales = "free") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.5)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "PPC Performance for Top-Performing Models",
       subtitle = "Better models appear in the upper-left corner",
       x = "Mean PPP Deviation from 0.5", 
       y = "Proportion of Good Fits (1 - Extreme Ratio)",
       color = "Model") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10)
  )
print(top_model_ppc_plot)

# Create a detailed analysis of parameter trade-offs
# Identify common parameters across models for comparison
common_params <- params_df %>%
  count(parameter) %>%
  filter(n >= length(available_all_models) / 2) %>%  # Parameter exists in at least half the models
  pull(parameter)

# Get parameter correlations for common parameters
param_comparison <- params_df %>%
  filter(parameter %in% common_params) %>%
  select(model, model_display, model_type, parameter, correlation) %>%
  spread(parameter, correlation)

# Display parameter trade-off table
kable(param_comparison, digits = 3,
      caption = "Parameter Recovery Trade-offs Across Models") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), 
                full_width = FALSE)

# Create a radar chart of parameter recovery for selected models
# Prep data for radar chart
radar_data <- params_df %>%
  filter(model %in% top_models, parameter %in% common_params) %>%
  select(model, model_display, parameter, correlation) %>%
  spread(parameter, correlation)

# Function to create a basic radar chart
create_radar_chart <- function(data, id_column, value_columns) {
  # Convert to long format
  long_data <- data %>%
    gather(key = "parameter", value = "correlation", all_of(value_columns))
  
  # Calculate angle for each parameter
  n_params <- length(value_columns)
  angles <- seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = n_params + 1)
  
  # Add row for plot closure
  plot_data <- long_data %>%
    group_by(!!sym(id_column)) %>%
    mutate(
      angle = angles[match(parameter, value_columns)],
      hjust = ifelse(angle < pi, 0, 1),
      vjust = ifelse(angle < pi/2 | angle > 3*pi/2, 0, 1)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # Create the radar chart
  ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = angle, y = correlation, 
                         color = !!sym(id_column), 
                         group = !!sym(id_column))) +
    geom_line() +
    geom_point() +
    coord_polar() +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = angles,
      labels = c(value_columns, ""),
      limits = c(0, 2*pi)
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray", size = 0.3),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray", size = 0.3, linetype = "dashed")
    )
}

# Create radar chart if we have enough data
if (nrow(radar_data) > 0 && length(common_params) >= 3) {
  radar_chart <- create_radar_chart(radar_data, "model_display", common_params)
  radar_plot <- radar_chart + 
    labs(title = "Parameter Recovery Comparison for Top Models",
         subtitle = "Radar chart showing correlation values for common parameters",
         color = "Model") +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 10)
    )
  print(radar_plot)
}
```

```{r}

```
